title: Level 961
wikitext:
{{Warningbox|color=green|content= [[File:UnderRewriteUpdated.png|thumb|120px|link=]] '''There is a rewrite in progress.'''
'''Date:''' {{{date|''May 29th, 2026'}}}
'''Reason: ''' {{{reason|''SPaG''}}}
'''Author(s): ''' {{{author|''BenJudd''}}}
'''Rewriter(s): ''' {{{rewriter|''BenJudd''}}}
'''Progress: ''' {{{progress|''10%''}}} }} {{Class 0|color-h=0|color-s=0%|color-l=50%|hexagon=0|class=0|safety=Safe|security=Stable|entity=Devoid Of Harmful Entities}} '''Level 961''' also known as "A Fraction Of Reality" is the 962nd level of the Backrooms. It is also the primary host for "The Angel’s Cure". == Description == {{Blurimage|caption=An image of '''Level 961'''.|file=Liminal-spaces-i-found-at-night-v0-5pszj4sglw6d1.jpg}} '''Level 961''''s main area appears like an average neighborhood that could be encountered in [[The Frontrooms]]. The level is permanently stuck at midnight, making it a tiny bit difficult to effectively traverse. The main area is composed of 3 main streets and 9 side streets, completed with a variety of craftsman style homes. The main area is completely surrounded by seemingly infinite grassy mountains and hills and log cabins to the East, West, and North, with an Island and more mountains to the South. Wanderers may note that the layout of the streets will often loop around. Not only are the layout of the streets completely nonsensical, but some houses can even appear upside-down. Even though the level is stuck at midnight, clocks and calendars on this level still function regularly. Most of the houses in the main area of the level are occupied by Facelings, but fortunately, these entities are entirely docile. Far off in the mountains, there are log cabins that can be found stocked with long term habilitation resources, and supplies needed for more dangerous levels. This level can be considered as a "safety hub" for wanderers due to the abundance of long term survival resources, lack of harmful entities, and the safety and stability of the level. Various phenomena are known to occur here. These phenomenons either have little to no effect on the wanderer or positive effects. One such phenomenon is "Relief" where upon entrance, the wanderer will calm down and any stress and psychological troubles that the wanderer may have experienced on previous levels are instantly relieved. The true scale of Level 961 is yet to be determined, as the mountains surrounding the level are seemingly never-ending. Since there are very little ways to traverse the level outside of walking, there's almost no real way to find what lies beyond the mountains. It is rumored that an entire city lies beyond. == Events == === The Blackout Event === The Blackout Event is where the entire level is engulfed in pure darkness for around 5 minutes at the longest. The moon will temporarily disappear, so there is absolutely 0 light whatsoever. Very rarely, the blackout can exude a hazard that renders wanderers temporarily blind. There is currently no info on why this event occurs. This event is extremely rare though, as the chances of it actually occurring are approximately 0.00009563%. Make sure one bring a flashlight or any light source just in case. Unlike the Firework event, there is no indicator for if or when this event might occur. It is completely random. === The Firework Event === The Firework Event is an event that occurs only under certain conditions; * The date is July 4th * The date is either [[Level 224|December 31st]] or January 1st During this event, all wanderers are instantly summoned to the center of The Main Area to watch Fireworks go off. It is unknown who is lighting the Fireworks or how they were obtained. There will be an indicator of the event when one can see either an American flag on a TV with the Star Spangled Banner playing, or if oner TV switched to footage of the ball dropping. These indicators will happen at least 48 hours before the event starts. If one isn't near a TV, then they'll hear one firework go off. == Other Areas == ==='''The Island'''=== {{Blurimage|file=TheIslandfrom962.jpeg|caption=The Island}} '''The Island''' can be found south of the main area. The Island's existence is questionable considering that the rest of the level is a neighborhood. Very rarely, the water will turn a different color. This phenomenon is still unexplained. The island is also surrounded by infinite grassy mountains, and a lake. The water that surrounds the island exhibits a rare phenomenon. === ''The Angel's Cure'' === This is a phenomenon where entering the lake will rid the wanderer of any injury and illness/mental hazard inflicted upon them on previous levels. Once cured, the wanderer is essentially invincible and invulnerable for only 1 week. For this week, there is nothing that can harm the wanderer, both physically and mentally, nor can the wanderer be killed. However, there is a limit to this phenomenon; This phenomenon only works once every 7 times one enter. In other words, one would have the exit and enter this level 7 times before the phenomenon works again. Due to this limitation, it is imperative that the wanderer does not enter levels that can harm them physically or mentally after the week of invulnerability. Measures are being taken to explore the properties of this lake in hope that there will be a way to get rid of this limitation, and replicate the properties of the water for mass production across The Backrooms. It is theorized that this phenomenon is connected to Archangel Wyatt. There is almost nothing that "The Angel's Cure" cannot cure. Even [[Level 959|that]] can be cured by it. === '''The Mountains''' === The Mountains are the area surrounding both the "Main Area" and "The Island". Other than log cabins, The Mountains are virtually empty with a very small amount of wanderers living in them. Nothing is known about what may or may not lie beyond them. Strangely enough, there are 0 existing photos of the mountains. == Entities & Hazards == === Facelings === The [[Facelings]] on this level are extremely docile and have 0 chance of committing any harm towards wanderers. === Black Eyed Hazard === There is an extremely rare hazard that can make a wanderer completely blind. There is only a 0.00068% chance of this actually occurring. This hazard lasts from 2-3 hours. The only real worry is the fact that the effect does not go away if one leave the level (if one can even leave, as they are blind after all), so a wanderer won't know whether or not they left until after the effect stops. This can be really bad if a wanderer were to somehow exit to a dangerous level. This is the only hazard that cannot be cured by The Angel's Cure. An important thing to note is that this hazard can only occur during The Blackout Event. == '''Outposts, Bases and Colonies''' == There is only one known base on '''Level 961,''' though the population of the level is approximately 4,000 wanderers. '''[[M.E.G.]] Base A.F.O.R''' *Has a member count of 700. *Guides lost wanderers. *Open to trade. == Entrances and Exits == ==='''Entrances'''=== There are only 2 guaranteed and consistent ways to enter this level. Any other entrance found is not consistent. *An extremely rare exit door on [[Level 960]] will send one here. *No-clipping into a mirror on a wall that can be found in [[Level 0]] will send one here. ==== Unconfirmed Entrances ==== * Although inconsistent and not guaranteed, there's a very rare chance that no-clipping at exactly midnight on [[Level 330]] will send one here. * [[Level 959|GET OUT OF THERE! AND HURRY FAST! No clip through the stairs! DO IT BEFORE IT GETS WORSE!]] ==='''Exits'''=== There are only 4 guaranteed and consistent ways to leave this level. Any other exit found is not consistent. * No-clipping through one of the main roads will take one to [[Level 79]]. This is the easiest and most common exit. * No-clipping during "The Firework Event" will take one to [[Level 224]]. This exit can only occur under certain conditions. * No-clipping during your week of invulnerability will take one straight to [[Level 993]]. This exit can only occur under certain conditions. * No-clipping into an out of place yellow wall in any of the houses will take one back to [[Level 0]]. ==== Unconfirmed Exits ==== * When one tries to no-clip to [[Level 993]], there is a rare chance of being randomly sent to [[Level 180]] instead, though the chances of this happening are astronomically low. * Wandering far enough in the mountains to the east or west has a small chance to gradually fade into either [[Level 10]] or [[Level 83]] depending on direction (10 to the east, 83 to the west) * There is a small chance that if one sleeps on The Island, it will turn into [[Level 87]] * Wandering far enough in the mountains to the north has a 50/50 chance of sending one [[The Frontrooms|home]]. Or it can gradually fade into [[Level 180]] ---- {{Redirect|Prev=960|Next=962}} [[Category:Levels]] [[Category:Safe]] [[Category:Cluster I]] [[Category:Safe Haven]]
